module_role: Module 3 of v17 integration — canonical 6-step BG battle resolution per mass_battle_v30 §B.3 + military_layer §2.1-§2.2 outcome margin table. Tactic card disposition handling per §B.4 (4 shared cards mechanically implemented, 16 faction-specific registered for routing). BattleResult dataclass with structured outcome (winner, damage, territory transfer, accord/stability/military deltas, route triggers). Pure primitives — does not mutate world state.

entries.quoted_text: Pool = sum of all engaged unit Martial values + commander bonus. Commander bonus = floor(faction Military / 2). [PP-555] TN 7.
Attacker net ≥ Defender net + 2 | Attacker wins | Territory captured; Defender Military −1
Formation Break → Discipline check Ob 2 → Route on fail.
Attacker wins | Territory captured; Defender Military −1 ... Defender wins | No territory change; Attacker Military −1
Partial | Margin ≤ 1 either direction | No territory change; Attacker Stability −1 (commitment cost)
Territory gained via BG Battle: Accord set to 1 (Resistant). See peninsular_strain_v1.md §2.4.
Defender loses Accord −1 in defended territory (war came to their home).
Tactic card disposition sets Ob (base Ob 2 for Standard Advance vs. Standard Advance)
Each faction receives a hand of 6 tactic cards at game start (4 shared + 2 faction-specific). Shared tactic cards (4): Standard Advance | Disciplined Defence | Feigned Retreat | Concentrated Strike
Faction-specific cards (2 per faction): 8 factions × 2 cards = 16. Crown | Church | Hafenmark | Varfell | Guilds | Niflhel | Löwenritter | Revolution. Tactic cards confirmed canonical (PP-283).
Standard Advance | Offensive | No additional effect
Disciplined Defence | Defensive | If opponent plays Offensive or Brutal: +1D Defence this engagement
Feigned Retreat | Balanced | On loss: opponent's winning units are Overextended (−2D next season in same territory)
Concentrated Strike | Offensive | One unit of your choice rolls +2D this engagement
Church | Crusade Fervour (Brutal + Discipline check exempt this turn) | Inquisitor's Mark (target unit −2D, any opponent)

Battle pool = Σ(Martial of all engaged unit tokens) + floor(faction Military / 2). Each unit token physically present in the territory contributes its Martial value. Commander bonus = floor(Military stat / 2) — this is the generalship abstraction (PP-555). Fort Level adds bonus dice to the defending pool (per existing rule).

entries.sim_variable: Fort dice apply to defender pool
entries.value: Bonus dice to defender pool (not Ob modifier)
entries.quoted_text: Fort bonus dice to defender: applies to the defender's pool, not as Ob. A Fort 3 territory gives the defender +3 dice on top of their Martial sum + commander bonus. This represents fortifications giving defenders a material advantage in the engagement, not making attackers less accurate.

Attacker wins | Attacker net ≥ Defender net + 2 | Territory captured; Defender Military −1. Partial | Margin ≤ 1 either direction | No territory change; Attacker Stability −1. Defender wins | Defender net ≥ Attacker net + 2 | No territory change; Attacker Military −1.

entries.value: defender +1D if attacker plays offensive or brutal disposition
entries.quoted_text: If opponent plays Offensive or Brutal: +1D Defence this engagement

entries.value: Sort defender units by Martial ascending; allocate damage to weakest first; net_successes × dmg_mod = total damage in Health units
entries.quoted_text: Apply damage. Reduce Health per Step 3 net successes × unit damage modifier − DR. Formation Break at Health 0.
entries.assumption: M3_ASSUMPTION_THREE: deterministic AI policy — attacker prioritizes lowest-Martial defender units (weakest-first). Canon §B.3 Step 5 says 'attacker chooses which units take damage'; M3 implements one canonical policy and surfaces the choice for override.

entries.value: pool of d6 = Discipline, success ≥4, route on successes < Ob 2
entries.quoted_text: Formation Break → Discipline check Ob 2 → Route on fail.
entries.note: BW success threshold (≥4 per d6) per mc_v15 quasibinomial_successes convention. Theoretical route rates: Levy (D=1) always routes (cannot reach Ob 2 with 1 die), LightInf/Archer (D=3) ~50%, HeavyInf (D=4) ~31%, Cavalry (D=5) ~19%, KnightsTemplar (D=6) ~11%.

entries.quoted_text: BW success threshold: each d6 succeeds on 4+ for routine rolls. mass_battle §B.3 'TN 7' = Difficulty 7 sets pool's total success Ob, not per-die threshold. Per-die threshold is the BW standard ≥4.

entries.value: {outcome, winner, attacker_net, defender_net, margin, attacker_pool_total, defender_pool_total, territory_transferred, attacker_losses, defender_losses, route_triggers, attacker_stability_delta, attacker_military_delta, defender_military_delta, accord_changes, side_effects}
entries.quoted_text: Outputs: BattleResult(winner, attacker_losses, defender_losses, territory_transferred, accord_outcome, stability_triggers)

provisional_assumptions.decision: M3 registers all 16 faction-specific tactic cards (§B.4 table) with name, faction, and disposition. Mechanical effects (Crown's Royal Guard '+3D Elite', Church's Crusade Fervour 'Discipline check exempt', Varfell's Stratagem 'read opposing card first', etc.) are NOT implemented in M3.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: Faction-specific mechanics belong to M6 Faction Action Expansion. M3 stays the primitives layer. The disposition routing (e.g. Crusade Fervour = Brutal triggers Disciplined Defence bonus) IS implemented in M3.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: M6 must implement faction-specific card effects as overlay modifications to BattleResult or pre-resolve hooks. Specifically Stratagem (initiative inversion at tactic card layer) requires a two-pass resolution that M3's current architecture does not support.

provisional_assumptions.topic: Dice rolls deterministic with explicit RNG
provisional_assumptions.decision: resolve_battle accepts an optional rng parameter (random.Random instance). Default uses system entropy. Tests pass seeded instances for byte-identical replays.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: Matches mc_v15 sim discipline. Required for sim_gate's reproducibility check and N=1000 balance sweeps in M7.

provisional_assumptions.decision: apply_damage_lowest_martial_first implements one canonical attacker policy: strike weakest defender unit first. Total damage = net_successes × dmg_mod (in Health units). Reduce defender units in Martial-ascending order until damage exhausted or no defender units remain.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: Canon §B.3 Step 5 says 'attacker chooses which units take damage'; in an automated sim, this requires a deterministic policy. Weakest-first matches historical doctrine (Vegetius: 'rout the enemy's weakest division'). Other policies (preserve-strongest-defender, scatter, target-cavalry-first) are valid alternative AI policies.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: M6 or M7 may override with faction-specific policies (e.g. Church Crusade Fervour might target Anti-Armour units first to neutralise siege threat). Surface as callable parameter.

provisional_assumptions.topic: Disposition table simplified to pool modifiers
provisional_assumptions.decision: Tactic card dispositions interact at the pool-modifier level: Disciplined Defence vs Offensive/Brutal → defender +1D pool; Concentrated Strike → attacker +2D pool; Feigned Retreat → no immediate pool change but flags overextended_next_season on winner if loser played it. Ob remains DISPOSITION_BASE_OB = 2 across all combinations.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: §B.3 Step 2 says 'Read attacker's row, defender's column. Apply Ob and dice modifier.' Without the full disposition matrix (which §B.3 inherits from 'existing disposition table B6' — a v3 doc artifact not in current canon), the M3 implementation expresses each tactic card's effect as direct pool modifiers, matching §B.4's per-card 'Additional effect' column.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: If a complete disposition table B6 surfaces in canon, M3 should be updated to apply Ob modifiers per disposition pair. Currently this is a known divergence from the §B.3 Step 2 wording in favour of the §B.4 effect-per-card semantics.

provisional_assumptions.topic: Morale check mechanic + Formation Break trigger
provisional_assumptions.decision: Discipline check fires per unit class that took any losses (not on 'Formation Break at Health 0' strictly, since M3 operates on aggregate counts not per-unit health). Mechanic: roll a d6 pool of size Discipline, count successes on ≥4, route on successes < DISCIPLINE_CHECK_OB (2). Levy (D=1) always routes; HeavyInf (D=4) routes ~31% per Binomial(4, 0.5) tail.
provisional_assumptions.rationale: M4's aggregate-count semantics (M4_ASSUMPTION_FIVE) don't track per-unit Health. The canonical Formation-Break-at-Health-0 trigger is approximated by 'any unit class that lost units this engagement makes a Discipline check.' This is conservative — every loss potentially triggers route.
provisional_assumptions.follow_up: If per-unit Health tracking is added in a later module, refine the trigger condition. Until then, the every-loss-checks-route policy is the canonical interpretation that fits aggregate-count semantics.
